creator: bf1783@googlemail.com
text:
>opinion against
>http://docs.freebsd.org/cgi/mid.cgi?AANLkTinqr_1Vj8koHh1vojterWJtri-izNVBZB_pEpkr

In the above post, I wasn't saying that patching ld (either in base or
in ports) to behave more like rtld in some ways is necessarily a bad
idea, I was just explaining that there had always been some
differences between the two -- because GNU ld is contributed software
designed to have consistent behavior across a wide number of different
platforms, and libmap.conf is a FreeBSD-specific extension to rtld
that was really only implemented to test different threading
libraries.  But if you want to patch ld, I think that you will
encounter some resistance from the people porting it, who will want to
minimize the number of changes that they have to make from the
original.

b.
